title: Script R1: Virome Contig and Sequencing Statistics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"enktj","text":"This protocol outlines the analysis used to plot contig coverage statistics, as well as sequence count and length stats. We begin with visualizing contig length vs coverage. We then visualize the distributions of sequence counts per sample as a probability density plot (similar idea as a histogram), and then do the same for median sequence length. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"97elf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"era60","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"481k5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"vvsa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2db9a","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4bcrb","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"akt7s","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4df5t","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpnec","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bacs2","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cp3si","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9lqp3","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahk78","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library( library("plyr") packageVersion("plyr") library("ggplot2") packageVersion("ggplot2") library("hexbin") packageVersion("hexbin") library("reshape2") packageVersion("reshape2") ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.27.0' ## [1] '1.4.1'

We will start by processing the virome samples. Import the tab delimited file containing the virome contig statistics. contig_stats contig_stats <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/contig_length_with_coverage_for_graphing.tsv", header=TRUE, sep='\t') head(contig_stats) %s %s ## contig count contig_length ## 1 34408_ 40 570 ## 2 578_ 26 648 ## 3 45993_ 44 668 ## 4 9954_ 360 2674 ## 5 66003_ 18 830 ## 6 28197_ 17 570

Generate a scatter plot comparing the number of mapped sequences to each contig, compared to the contig length. ggplot(contig_stats, aes(x=contig_length, y=count)) + theme_bw() + stat_binhex(bins=100)+ scale_fill_gradientn(colours=c(

Next we will estimate contig coverage, using a sequence length value of 150 bp. contig_stats$coverage contig_stats$coverage <- (contig_stats$count * 150) / contig_stats$contig_length head(contig_stats) %s %s ## contig count contig_length coverage ## 1 34408_ 40 570 10.526316 ## 2 578_ 26 648 6.018519 ## 3 45993_ 44 668 9.880240 ## 4 9954_ 360 2674 20.194465 ## 5 66003_ 18 830 3.253012 ## 6 28197_ 17 570 4.473684
Like above, we will plot the contig coverage against the contig length. ggplot(contig_stats, aes(x=contig_length, y=coverage)) + theme_bw() + stat_binhex(bins=100)+ scale_fill_gradientn(colours=c(

Next we can calculate the sequence statistics by anatomical site. First import the sequence summary statistics table. Be sure to change the column names of the input data frames, and to merge the data frames together for downstream processing. Here we will also import and format the mapping file. #Import the sequence counts before quality controlINPUT_RAW #Import the sequence counts before quality control INPUT_RAW <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/raw_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_RAW) <- c("SampleID","Raw")

Now we can caluclate the sequence statistics by site and plot the sequence count information after some more formatting and merging. MERGE_SUBSET MERGE_SUBSET <- MERGE_MAP[,c(2:5,1,10,11)] MERGE_SUB_RAW <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Raw","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_RAW) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_RAW$GROUP <- "Raw" MERGE_SUB_TRIM <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Trim","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_TRIM) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_TRIM$GROUP <- "Trim" MERGE_SUB_HUMAN <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Human","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_HUMAN) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_HUMAN$GROUP <- "Human" MERGE_SUB_NEG <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Neg","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_NEG) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_NEG$GROUP <- "Neg" CAT_MERGE <- rbind(MERGE_SUB_RAW, MERGE_SUB_TRIM, MERGE_SUB_HUMAN, MERGE_SUB_NEG)

Now plot the sequence count information. ggplot(CAT_MERGE_SUB, aes(x=Count, fill=GROUP)) + theme_bw() + geom_density(alpha=0.2) + scale_x_log10() + facet_wrap(~Site_Symbol, scale= ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 4 rows containing non-finite values (stat_density).

Now we are going to do the same thing as the above sequence count stats, except now we are using the sequence length stats. LENGTH_RAW LENGTH_RAW <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/raw_sequence_length_medians.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_RAW) <- c("Raw","SampleID") LENGTH_TRIM <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_sequence_length_medians.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_TRIM) <- c("Trim","SampleID") LENGTH_HUMAN <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/clean_sequence_length_medians.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_HUMAN) <- c("Human","SampleID") LENGTH_NEG <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/negative_clean_sequence_length_medians.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(LENGTH_NEG) <- c("Neg","SampleID") LENGTH_MERGE <- merge(merge(merge(LENGTH_RAW, LENGTH_TRIM, by="SampleID"), LENGTH_HUMAN, by="SampleID"), LENGTH_NEG, by="SampleID", all=TRUE) MERGE_MAP <- merge(LENGTH_MERGE, INPUT_MAP, by.x="SampleID", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID")

Plot the sequence count information. ggplot(CAT_MERGE_SUB, aes(x=Count, fill=GROUP)) + theme_bw() + geom_density(alpha=0.2) + facet_wrap(~Site_Symbol, scale= ## Warning in loop_apply(n, do.ply): Removed 4 rows containing non-finite values (stat_density).

Import the sequence counts after sequence quality control. INPUT_TRIM INPUT_TRIM <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/trimmed_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_TRIM) <- c("SampleID","Trim")

Import the sequence counts after human sequence decontamination. INPUT_HUMAN INPUT_HUMAN <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/human_deconseq_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_HUMAN) <- c("SampleID","Human")

Import the sequence counts after environmental background has been removed. INPUT_NEG INPUT_NEG <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Virome_Sequence_Counts/negative_clean_sequence_counts.tsv", header=FALSE, sep="\t") colnames(INPUT_NEG) <- c("SampleID","Neg")

Merge the input file so they are easier to deal with downstream. INPUT_MERGE INPUT_MERGE <- merge(merge(merge(INPUT_RAW, INPUT_TRIM, by="SampleID"), INPUT_HUMAN, by="SampleID"), INPUT_NEG, by="SampleID", all=TRUE) INPUT_MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE)

Merge the mapping file and merged data frame. MERGE_MAP MERGE_MAP <- merge(INPUT_MERGE, INPUT_MAP, by.x="SampleID", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") head(MERGE_MAP)[,c(1:5)] %s %s ## SampleID Raw Trim Human Neg ## 1 MG100098 188574 185953 169740 90875 ## 2 MG100099 238653 3263 3193 2094 ## 3 MG100100 211300 155254 148954 57638 ## 4 MG100101 55803 21080 20964 14366 ## 5 MG100102 169504 77972 74997 34095 ## 6 MG100103 105538 38790 35722 18480

Check the head of this data frame. head(CAT_MERGE_SUB) %s %s ## Count SampleID Site_Symbol Group ## 1 188574 MG100098 Fh Raw ## 2 238653 MG100099 Ra Raw ## 3 211300 MG100100 Oc Raw ## 4 55803 MG100101 Ax Raw ## 5 169504 MG100102 Ac Raw ## 6 363647 MG100104 Pa Raw

Prepare format to plot sequence length. MERGE_SUBSET MERGE_SUBSET <- MERGE_MAP[,c(2:5,1,10,11)] MERGE_SUB_RAW <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Raw","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_RAW) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_RAW$GROUP <- "Raw" MERGE_SUB_TRIM <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Trim","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_TRIM) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_TRIM$GROUP <- "Trim" MERGE_SUB_HUMAN <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Human","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_HUMAN) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_HUMAN$GROUP <- "Human" MERGE_SUB_NEG <- MERGE_SUBSET[,c("Neg","SampleID","Site_Symbol")] colnames(MERGE_SUB_NEG) <- c("Count","SampleID","Site_Symbol") MERGE_SUB_NEG$GROUP <- "Neg" CAT_MERGE <- rbind(MERGE_SUB_RAW, MERGE_SUB_TRIM, MERGE_SUB_HUMAN, MERGE_SUB_NEG) CAT_MERGE_SUB <- CAT_MERGE[-which(CAT_MERGE$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","No")), ]
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
